one_time_description: Indigenous Services Canada forgiveness of Treaty Loan of $5,663,069 representing advances made by Canada for negotiations under the First Nation Negotiation Support Agreements
economic_notes: Nation is developing a reserve on York Road in Campbell River (Nenagwas Community Development). Investments in fishing licence/quota businesses, forestry/timber operations, carbon credit businesses, and Grizzly bear tour lodge (Knight Inlet Lodge). COVID-19 noted as subsequent event likely to have material impact on 2021 surplus.
notes: Fiscal year 2020, not 2024. Large one-time item: $5,663,069 ISC Treaty Loan forgiven, significantly inflating annual surplus to $8,070,029. Four fully consolidated subsidiaries (Chief's Pride Aquaculture Corporation, Chief's Pride Seafoods Inc., 0834472 B.C. Ltd., Happy as a Clam Shellfish Ltd.) and seven GBEs/partnerships accounted for under modified equity method. Prior period adjustments restated 2019 comparatives. BC Treaty Commission funding ($278,326) included in Provincial revenue line in segment disclosure. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($922,682) plus Province of BC ($196,132) plus BC Treaty Commission ($278,326) = $1,396,808. Honoraria of $113,532 paid suggesting council/chief remuneration but no formal schedule provided. No schedule of remuneration disclosed.
